experiment: Joint OSOTSS x Abs-Conformal SRPT Compound Backtest
hypothesis: OSOTSS under-provisions vs AMCSG (-5.6% GPU-hours), creating deeper queues that make conformal SRPT more valuable -> positive compound over conformal+AMCSG
methodology: 2x3 factorial: {FIFO, conformal} x {fixed-c=4, AMCSG gate=12.5%, OSOTSS}. Provisioned GPU-hour cost model (no stochastic spot interruptions). Both capacity schedules computed via canonical AureliusOptimizer policy delegates.
five_failure_rule_status: TRIGGERED (5/5). This is integration work: combines existing compute_mcs_c_schedule, compute_online_sotss_schedule, _simulate_fifo_variable_c, _simulate_abs_conformal_variable_c.
finding_summary: Conformal SRPT has a NEGATIVE interaction with variable-c capacity scheduling (both AMCSG and OSOTSS). Conformal+AMCSG < FIFO+AMCSG (-2.4% Azure, -3.8% BurstGPT) and conformal+OSOTSS < FIFO+OSOTSS (-4.0% Azure, -6.4% BurstGPT). The hypothesis that deeper queues under OSOTSS would benefit conformal SRPT was REFUTED. Preemption overhead under capacity changes creates request starvation that eliminates the conformal advantage. The strongest combination remains FIFO+OSOTSS (= existing frontier, 63,831 gp/$ on Azure provisioned-cost scale).
mechanism: Conformal SRPT preempts long jobs in favor of short ones. Under variable-c, when capacity drops, preempted long jobs accumulate wait time on top of the service time penalty, causing SLA violations. AMCSG/OSOTSS already handles the utilization; adding preemptive priority on top hurts. At fixed-c=4, conformal SRPT is powerful (+313% Azure) because the bottleneck is queue ordering. With AMCSG/OSOTSS, the bottleneck is capacity allocation and conformal preemption creates overhead.

azure.note: Conformal SRPT REDUCES n_sla_safe vs FIFO under variable-c (both AMCSG and OSOTSS)

burstgpt.note: Same pattern, larger gap. OSOTSS BurstGPT gap persists under provisioned-cost model.

architecture_insight: Conformal SRPT value is context-dependent: excellent at fixed-c (queue-ordering bottleneck) but harmful under variable-c (capacity-change bottleneck). These two dimensions compete rather than compound. The serving_queue and replica_scaling policies occupy different regimes and should NOT be combined via preemptive ordering.
reason_not_merged: NULL RESULT: conformal+OSOTSS does not beat FIFO+OSOTSS baseline. The correct frontier combination is FIFO+OSOTSS (existing result). No new module added; experiment is benchmark infrastructure only.
